back_translation: The men of Kiriath-jearim came and brought up the ark of the LORD, and they brought it to the house of Abinadab on the hill, and Eleazar his son they consecrated to guard the ark of the LORD.
It was from the day of the ark's dwelling at Kiriath-jearim, and the days multiplied, and they were twenty years, and all the house of Israel mourned after the LORD.

Samuel said to all the house of Israel, saying, 'If with all your heart you are returning to the LORD, put away the gods of the foreigner from your midst, and the Ashtoreths, and prepare your hearts to the LORD, and serve him alone — and he will deliver you from the hand of the Philistines.'
The sons of Israel put away the Baals and the Ashtoreths, and they served the LORD alone.
Samuel said, 'Gather all Israel to Mizpah, and I will pray to the LORD for you.'
They gathered at Mizpah and drew water and poured out before the LORD, and they fasted on that day, and they said there, 'We have sinned to the LORD.' And Samuel judged the sons of Israel at Mizpah.
The Philistines heard that the sons of Israel had gathered at Mizpah, and the rulers of the Philistines went up against Israel, and the sons of Israel heard, and they feared before the Philistines.
The sons of Israel said to Samuel, 'Do not be silent from us from crying out to the LORD our God, that he may save us from the hand of the Philistines.'
Samuel took one suckling lamb and offered it as a whole burnt offering to the LORD, and Samuel cried out to the LORD for Israel, and the LORD answered him.

It was as Samuel was offering up the burnt offering, the Philistines drew near for war against Israel — and the LORD thundered in a great voice on that day against the Philistines and confounded them, and they were defeated before Israel.
The men of Israel went out from Mizpah and pursued the Philistines, and they struck them as far as beneath Beth-car.
Samuel took one stone and set it between Mizpah and between Shen, and he called its name Ebenezer, and said, 'Hitherto the LORD has helped us.'
The Philistines were subdued, and they did not again enter the territory of Israel, and the hand of the LORD was against the Philistines all the days of Samuel.

The cities that the Philistines took from Israel returned to Israel — from Ekron and as far as Gath — and Israel delivered their territory from the hand of the Philistines, and there was peace between Israel and between the Amorites.
Samuel judged Israel all the days of his life.
He went from year to year, and went round Bethel and the Gilgal and the Mizpah, and judged Israel in all these places.
His returning was to Ramah, for there was his house, and there he judged Israel, and he built there an altar to the LORD.
